description:
Goal: make the production-facing Compiler2 backend path the real one and remove temporary compiler2-side fallback. Signal: CLI/test/support entry points that opt into Compiler2 consume the new interpreter/JIT/AOT paths, and no Compiler2 path quietly falls back to old planner/codegen discovery.

Change:
- Switch Compiler2-facing entry points, helpers, and test support onto the new interpreter/JIT/AOT consumers.
- Remove temporary compiler2-only dual-path helpers or leftover fallback hooks added during migration.
- Update docs and regression harnesses to point at the new backend path and rerun surface.

Why:
- Until the outer entry points switch, the backend migration stays theoretical and dead fallback code can keep architectural regressions hidden.

Checks:
- Compiler2 entry points use the new backend consumers end-to-end.
- No Compiler2 path reaches back into old planner/codegen discovery helpers to fill missing facts.
title: Compiler2 backends: switch entry points and remove compiler2 fallback
